identity.title: Generate the pinned compatibility inventory and executable oracle corpus

authorization.reference: Operator explicitly instructed Codex to proceed and activate WP-FF-004 after WP-FF-003 v2 was implemented, validated, and hardened.

lifecycle.next_action: Implement the versioned oracle manifest, generated compatibility profile, deterministic corpus partitions, fail-closed validators, differential reporting, negative fixtures, and no-context commands through the validated WP-FF-003 v2 tooling surface.

scope.summary: Create a generated, versioned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 compatibility profile and executable oracle corpus that enumerates behavior and provides deterministic comparison planes without making yt-dlp or Python a production dependency.
in_scope: Generate option, alias, configuration, default, preset, interaction, extractor, and URL-class inventories from the pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 oracle.
Define separate Ferric-native semantics and yt-dlp compatibility-profile semantics so neither silently redefines the other.
Version source-graph, normalized result, sanitized network-transcript, filesystem/process artifact, error, archive, and migration oracle planes.
Create representative mandatory deterministic corpus partitions plus an explicitly opt-in live-canary policy.
Record normalization versions, provenance, artifact hashes, expected outcomes, intentional divergences, nondeterminism controls, and secret sanitization.
Implement machine-readable inventory/corpus validation and differential reporting through the accepted WP-003 tooling surface.
Update the Phase 0 no-context model manual with the implemented generation, replay, comparison, and recovery commands.
non_goals: Claiming full replacement parity before every mandatory row passes or has an explicit accepted divergence.
Implementing Ferric extractors, transport fingerprints, JavaScript execution, archive storage, or broad CLI compatibility.
Treating live network results as deterministic corpus proof.
Silently narrowing the final extractor, protocol, collection, archive, metadata, sink, or compatibility scope.
dependencies: WP-FF-003-executable-gate-bootstrap-v2 VALIDATED with verified main containment
Pinned yt-dlp 2026.07.04 source and executable oracle
Accepted WP-003 architecture, tooling, report, and negative-fixture conventions

acceptance_matrix.requirement: A generator produces a versioned machine-readable profile for yt-dlp 2026.07.04 covering options, aliases, config/default/preset interactions, extractor descriptors, and URL classes, with pinned provenance and deterministic regeneration proof.

A versioned mandatory corpus manifest covers source graph, normalized observation, sanitized transcript, artifact, failure, archive, and migration planes; each case has a stable ID, oracle identity, normalization version, expected result, and deterministic/live classification.

Executable validation rejects missing inventory rows, duplicate or unstable IDs, unsanitized secret fixtures, unpinned oracle inputs, unknown normalization versions, and deterministic cases that depend on live network state.

Differential reporting classifies each observed difference as Ferric defect, accepted baseline correction, nondeterministic response, accepted divergence with stable decision ID, or missing feature; no silent omission counts as pass.
The canonical architecture and PR gates pass on the pinned toolchain, and packet-specific corpus checks emit a machine-readable report linked to these acceptance IDs.
The Phase 0 model manual explains generation, offline replay, opt-in live canaries, inputs, outputs, sanitization, failure recovery, and the rule that corpus evidence is not a parity claim by itself.

repository_layout.runtime_boundary: Shipped product execution must require neither .GOV/ nor build/.

stub_implementation_brief.base_scope: Produce the Phase 0A compatibility inventory and executable oracle foundation required to measure native Ferric behavior against the pinned yt-dlp baseline.

implementation_readiness_gate_items: Generated versioned yt-dlp compatibility profile and mandatory corpus
Executable phase gates with machine-readable reports
No-context model manual, structured diagnostics, stable identifiers, and parallel-agent-safe receipts

promotion_requirements: Verify the exact yt-dlp 2026.07.04 source/artifact identity and generator inputs against current primary sources and pinned repository evidence.
Inspect WP-003 outputs and use its accepted report schemas, fixture layout, validator command conventions, and architecture policy instead of creating parallel infrastructure.
Record current research sources, inspected upstream implementation paths, selected and rejected generation approaches, sanitization threats, corpus sampling rationale, and validation plan.
Replace proposed output paths or command names only where the validated WP-003 topology requires it; update allowed_paths before implementation.
planned_outputs: Versioned mandatory corpus manifest with stable case IDs
Sanitized deterministic fixtures and opt-in live-canary manifest
Machine-readable corpus validation and differential reports

high_roi_additions.addition: Generate inventory-diff reports between pinned profile versions.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The same parser and stable IDs already required for generation can expose added, removed, and changed behavior cheaply.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents silent compatibility drift and makes future yt-dlp baseline updates reviewable.
high_roi_additions.reuse: WP-003 report envelope, profile generator, stable IDs, and corpus manifest.
high_roi_additions.verification: Mutation fixture adds/removes/changes one upstream row and the diff report must identify it exactly.

high_roi_additions.addition: Add a sanitizer validator with secret canaries and allowlisted normalized tokens.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Sanitized transcripts are already required; testing the sanitizer while fixtures are built avoids later corpus rewrites and leakage.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents credentials, cookies, tokens, and machine-local data from entering committed fixtures or reports.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Transcript normalization pipeline and negative-fixture convention from WP-003.
high_roi_additions.verification: Seed representative secret forms and prove validation fails before any fixture is accepted.

high_roi_additions.addition: Shard corpus manifests by stable case ID with deterministic merge receipts.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Partition metadata is cheap when stable IDs are first defined and enables parallel models to generate or replay disjoint cases safely.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Reduces merge conflicts, duplicate work, and untraceable partial corpus results.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Stable case IDs, machine-readable reports, and packet/Git recovery conventions.
high_roi_additions.verification: Two disjoint shard reports merge deterministically; overlap, missing shards, and mismatched corpus versions fail.

high_roi_additions.addition: Keep deterministic offline corpus and opt-in live canaries as mechanically separate suites.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The classification is already required in every case and makes CI reliable without losing current-site signal.
high_roi_additions.gap_or_risk_closed: Prevents network nondeterminism from masquerading as regression proof or blocking ordinary development.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Corpus manifest classification and WP-003 gate selection.
high_roi_additions.verification: Default checks prove no live network access; an explicit live command emits a distinct non-deterministic report.

existing_systems_to_reuse: WP-003 architecture policy, rule-to-proof map, and negative fixtures
Technical design Sections 20, 26, 29, and 31 compatibility classifications
gaps_closed: Compatibility target becomes enumerable rather than aspirational.
Oracle comparison becomes executable and versioned.
Nondeterminism, accepted divergence, and missing behavior become explicit machine states.
Future extractors and spikes receive reusable cases instead of inventing ad hoc fixtures.

risk_scenarios.risk: Inventory generator silently misses dynamically registered or interaction-dependent behavior.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: The profile reports complete coverage while a pinned option, extractor, default interaction, or URL class is absent.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Combine source-level enumeration, runtime help/config inspection, independent counts, collision reports, and mutation fixtures; missing rows fail closed.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Cross-check generated counts and stable IDs against at least two independent pinned upstream surfaces and remove one known row to prove failure.

risk_scenarios.risk: Corpus overfits a narrow happy path.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Mandatory cases pass while redirects, failure semantics, artifacts, archive behavior, or config interactions remain untested.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Use explicit plane and behavior-dimension coverage matrices; require each mandatory row to map to cases or a recorded gap.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Coverage validator rejects an intentionally uncovered mandatory dimension and reports the exact missing mappings.

risk_scenarios.risk: Fixtures contain secrets or machine-specific state.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Cookies, tokens, absolute paths, timestamps, or host identifiers are committed and replay becomes unsafe or non-portable.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Normalize secrets and environmental values to deterministic tokens, scan before write and before commit, and keep credentials/live inputs outside committed artifacts.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Secret/path/time canary fixtures must be rejected or normalized identically across supported hosts.

risk_scenarios.risk: A newer yt-dlp or live response silently replaces the pinned oracle.
risk_scenarios.failure_scenario: Regeneration changes expected behavior without an explicit profile version or review.
risk_scenarios.mitigation_and_hardening: Pin artifact/source identity and hashes in manifests, make network use explicit, and require versioned inventory diffs for baseline changes.
risk_scenarios.verification_needed: Mismatched oracle version/hash and unexpected network access fail before generation or comparison.

verification_needs: JSON/schema validation and deterministic regeneration comparison
Secret, absolute-path, clock, randomness, and network negative probes
Mutation tests for omitted/duplicated/changed inventory rows
Offline differential replay with classified outcomes
no_context_execution_order: Read START_HERE.yaml, AGENTS.md, .GOV/codex.yaml, taskboard, this packet, topology, build rules, and only the cited design anchors/readiness items.
Confirm this packet is active and no longer STUB; confirm WP-003 is VALIDATED and refresh Git checkpoint fields.
Complete the research-backed refinement and validate the pinned oracle identity.
Define versioned schemas, stable IDs, coverage dimensions, normalization, sanitization, and deterministic/live partitions before collecting fixtures.
Implement generators and validators through fforager-xtask, then create corpus cases.
Run negative probes, deterministic regeneration, differential replay, canonical gates, and independent adversarial validation.
Update manual and packet proof, commit closure, set MERGE_PENDING only after acceptance, then prove main containment before VALIDATED.
